rows.description: Short-circuit: any general danger sign (convulsions, not able to drink/feed, vomiting everything, unusually sleepy/unconscious) or referral danger sign (chest indrawing, cough >=14d, fever >=7d, diarrhoea >=14d, blood in stool, MUAC red, bilateral foot oedema) triggers urgent referral pathway
MUAC red or bilateral foot oedema indicates severe malnutrition; refer urgently
Cough for 14 days or more is a danger sign; refer
Fever for 7 days or more is a danger sign; refer
Diarrhoea for 14 days or more is a danger sign; refer
Blood in stool (dysentery) is a danger sign; refer
Cough present and duration < 14 days; classify cough and check for fast breathing / pneumonia
Fever present and duration < 7 days; perform RDT and classify fever
Diarrhoea present and duration < 14 days without blood in stool; classify diarrhoea
Age-eligible child; assess MUAC and check for bilateral foot oedema
Fast breathing without chest indrawing; treat pneumonia with amoxicillin at home
RDT positive and no danger signs; treat malaria with AL at home
Diarrhoea without danger signs; treat with ORS and zinc at home
RDT result invalid; repeat test with new RDT
RDT negative with fever; do not give antimalarial, follow up in 3 days, refer if fever persists
MUAC yellow indicates moderate malnutrition; refer to supplementary feeding if available and provide home care advice
Follow-up visit at 3 days for any treated condition; reassess and refer if not improving
No illness signs and MUAC green; provide routine home care advice and counsel caregiver on when to return immediately
